text:
Level-5 ponders making Ushiro for Nintendo Switch 2, nearly 20 years after it was announced for PSP

It’s still looking like Level-5 won’t be bringing out Ushiro anytime soon, but in the latest update from the company, we’re hearing that Nintendo Switch 2 could be a possibility for the game… at some point. Maybe. For those that are unaware, Ushiro was originally announced almost twenty years ago for the PSP. Years have gone by, and while Level-5...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Under Night In-Birth 2 Sys:Celes getting Zohar as new DLC character

May 1: Today, Arc System Works and French-Bread made it known that they’re releasing Zohar as a new DLC character for Under Night In-Birth 2 Sys:Celes. Expect a release on Nintendo Switch this summer. Here’s what Arc System Works had to say about the announcement: “Claws encroaching unseen, the ‘Doppelganger’ finally joins as the 4th DLC fighter in Summer 2026....

text:
Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising announced for Nintendo Switch 2

Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising is making its way to Nintendo Switch 2, Cygames revealed today. It will be available on September 17, 2026. Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising is a fighting game. On the same day as the Nintendo Switch 2 version, the title is getting a version 2.0 update with “Id” as a DLC character, a new stage, new system...

text:
Nintendo issues June 2026 backwards compatibility fixes for Switch games on Switch 2, full list

Another set of backwards compatibility fixes for Nintendo Switch games on Switch 2 are now available.  There aren’t as many heavy hitters this time around, but it’s still a solid batch. Shovel Knight: Treasure Trove is the biggest name out of the bunch – previously, Nintendo said that players may encounter audio issues in some areas. The full list of...

text:
List of all prices for Nintendo Switch 2 Upgrade Packs

Various store pages confirm pricing for all Nintendo Switch 2 Upgrade Packs that will be available. There are a variety of price points, with some costing as much as $20. On the other hand, many are free – those these are from third-parties. Here’s the full roundup: 4PGP – $4.99 A-Train: All Aboard Tourism – $6.99 Animal Crossing: New Horizons...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo added over 300 permanent jobs in Fiscal Year 2026, plus other data

Nintendo updated its employee data this week, which reveals that it added over 300 permanent staff during Fiscal Year 2026. Information was recorded between April 2025 and March 2026. Last year, Nintendo’s headcount was at 5,630. This time around, the total increased to 5,938. We’re also seeing that the average tenure for a Nintendo employee is up to 14.6 years....

The post Nintendo added over 300 permanent jobs in Fiscal Year 2026, plus other data appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

text:
Deltarune 0.0.0242 update announced, patch notes

The second Deltarune update of the week has been announced with version 0.0.0242. Just like the previous one, it’s not out on Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch yet, but that’ll change in the near future. The update comes hot on the heels of the release of Chapter 5. A number of critical fixes have been implemented, including ones that address...

text:
Nintendo Switch eShop charts – June 27, 2026 – Rhythm Heaven coming for Tomodachi Life

The latest Nintendo Switch eShop charts for the week of June 27, 2026 are now available. For the most part, Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream has had a stranglehold on the top spot since launch. However, there’s now a chance that it’ll be dethroned. Rhythm Heaven Groove comes out next week, and with pre-loads alone, it’s already at #1. It’ll...

text:
The Drifter launch trailer – pulp adventure thriller for Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch

Powerhoof has a new launch trailer in for Powerhoof, which celebrates the new Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch versions. We just saw the pulp adventure thriller on both consoles. You can find more information about this one with the following details: The Nintendo Switch 2 Edition of The Drifter supports up to 4K and 120fps, delivering extra crisp pixel art...

text:
Pikmin 4 sound design lead talks about working on the game’s sound effects

In a post on Nintendo’s recruitment site, Pikmin 4 sound design lead Taiyo Furukawa reflected on his work for the game’s sound effects. This entry in the series shifts the camera to put players closer to the action, and that required a new type of approach. For example, we previously heard from Nintendo about how players can clearly hear Pikmin’s...

The post Pikmin 4 sound design lead talks about working on the game’s sound effects appeared first on Nintendo Everything.
